headline: Orchestration techniques cooled across the quarter while cost and token concerns broke out as the field's new pressure point.
body: Agent orchestration and model routing led the quarter on volume but both weakened, and AI-assisted development, cognitive architecture, and memory systems lagged behind. The breakouts shifted toward economics and efficiency: cost measurement, token optimization, training-free methods, and quantization all surfaced as outliers or new entrants. New items reinforce that turn, with a piece warning of token price increases ahead of AI IPOs [0], an analysis of capital spending against hypothetical returns [1], and a founder weighing the ROI of AI coding without venture backing [7]. Anxiety about AI's effect on engineering careers [10] and questions about how tunable thinking efforts are implemented [11] round out the window.

cited_items.title: Is this the dawn of the Tokenpocalypse?
Billions spent and hypothetical returns: the AI boom explained with six charts
The ROI of AI coding looks different when you are a bootstrapped founder
LLMs are eroding my software engineering career and I don't know what to do
Ask HN: How are thinking efforts implemented?
